chunk_summary: Defines extrauterine growth restriction (EUGR) and post-discharge growth failure in preterm/LBW/ELBW infants, emphasizing standardized definitions, severity grading, and actionable growth failure triggers for outpatient follow-up.
learning_objectives: Understand the definitions and criteria for extrauterine growth restriction (EUGR).
Learn how to assess and grade the severity of post-discharge growth failure.
Identify actionable triggers for reassessment and intervention in post-discharge growth monitoring.
Recognize the importance of balanced growth across weight, length, and head circumference.
key_facts: Cross-sectional EUGR is defined as weight, length, or head circumference <10th percentile or z-score <-1.28 at discharge or postmenstrual age.
Longitudinal EUGR involves a drop >1 z-score from birth to discharge, indicating significant centile crossing.
Severity grading: mild (z-score -1 to -2), moderate (-2 to -3), severe (<-3 or rapid decline across domains).
Post-discharge growth failure triggers include weight gain <15 g/day after term-equivalent age or a drop >1 z-score between visits.
“Weight-only recovery” flags suboptimal protein/mineral balance when weight improves but length or head growth lags.
reference_quote: Postnatal growth failure in preterm infants: epidemiology and outcomes — 'A drop >1 z-score from birth to discharge is a pragmatic trigger for concern, indicating significant centile crossing.'
document_key_topics: Extrauterine growth restriction (EUGR) definitions and criteria
Post-discharge growth monitoring and intervention triggers
